idioms.name: process_substitution
idioms.powershell_template:
$_output = & "{cmd}"
if ($_output) { parse KEY=VALUE and set variables }
idioms.notes: Most critical — appears in every gstack preamble. Requires explicit capture and parse.

idioms.name: default_value_use
idioms.notes: Use default if var is unset/empty. Inline vs statement form depends on context.

idioms.name: default_value_assign
idioms.notes: Assign default if var is unset/empty. Similar to :- but always assigns.

idioms.name: alternate_value
idioms.notes: Use alternate value if var IS set.

idioms.name: and_chain
idioms.notes: Run cmd2 only if cmd1 succeeds.

examples.description: Read config only if it exists

idioms.name: or_chain
idioms.notes: Run cmd2 only if cmd1 fails.

examples.bash: git status || echo 'Not a git repo'
examples.powershell: git status; if (-not $?) { Write-Output 'Not a git repo' }
examples.description: Fallback message if not in git repo

idioms.name: or_true
idioms.notes: Suppress command failure. Often used with source <(...).

idioms.name: redirect_stderr_null
idioms.notes: Suppress stderr output.

idioms.name: redirect_all_null
idioms.notes: Suppress all output (stdout and stderr).

idioms.name: redirect_all_null_alt
idioms.notes: Suppress all output (bash shorthand).

idioms.name: redirect_stdout_null
idioms.notes: Suppress stdout output.

idioms.name: test_file_exists
idioms.notes: Test if regular file exists.

idioms.name: test_dir_exists
idioms.notes: Test if directory exists.

idioms.name: test_path_exists
idioms.notes: Test if any path exists.

idioms.name: test_string_nonempty
idioms.notes: Test if string is non-empty.

idioms.name: test_string_empty
idioms.notes: Test if string is empty or null.

idioms.name: test_regex_match
idioms.notes: Regex match test (extended test syntax).

idioms.name: arithmetic_expansion
idioms.notes: Arithmetic evaluation. PowerShell handles math natively.

idioms.name: command_substitution
idioms.notes: Command substitution. Similar in PowerShell but may need cmdlet translation.

idioms.name: backtick_substitution
idioms.notes: Legacy backtick command substitution. Convert to $() form.

categories.structural: Patterns that change code structure significantly

transform_priorities.description: Order of transformation to avoid conflicts
